Read the chapter/section below from the module reading list to learn about this topic:

- Chapters 1, 3 and 4: Hwang, K (2017) Cloud Computing for Machine Learning and Cognitive Applications, MIT Press.

- [Section 2.4: Hwang, K (2017), Cloud Computing for Machine Learning and Cognitive Applications, MIT Press.](https://www.vlebooks.com/Product/Index/1991124)
- Section 1 (Chapters 1, 2 and 3): Purkait, N. (2019), Neural Networks with Keras, Packt.
- Section 2 (Chapters 4, 5, 6 and 7): Purkait, N. (2019), Neural Networks with Keras, Packt.
- [Chapters 8, 9 and 10: Purkait, N. (2019), Neural Networks with Keras, Packt.](https://www.vlebooks.com/Product/Index/1239038)

From <[https://app.qa.com/resource/topic-1-introduction-to-machine-learning-using-cloud-computing-ai-1698/?context_id=15065&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/resource/topic-1-introduction-to-machine-learning-using-cloud-computing-ai-1698/?context_id=15065&context_resource=lp)>

# Books

# Main

Cloud needs to be upgraded with

- ML capabilities
- IoT sensing
- Data analytics
- Cognitive power

Though

- Hardware virtualisation
- Machine learning
- Deep learning
- Neuromorphic computer architecture

Develop

- Learning as a Service(LaaS)
- Analytics as a Service(AaaS)
- Security as a service(SECaaS)

## What is cloud computing

Provision of off-premises computing service to consumers through the internet. Can access computing power, storage, databases, on an as-needed basis from a Cloud service provider.

Factors that paved the way to cloud computing.

- Broadband and wireless networking
- Falling storage costs
- Improvements in internet computing software
- Technological advancements in CPU, memory chips and disk arrays
- Resource virtualisation

![](data:image/png;base64,)

## Cloud service models

There are three main types of Cloud service models.

- Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS)
- Platform as a service(PaaS)
- Software as a service(SaaS)

IaaS provides virtualised computing resources such as VMs, storage and networking.

Users can run operating systems and applications on these virtualised resources as if they were on physical hardware.

e.g. AWS, Mazure, Google Compute Engine, Digital Ocean

PaaS. Applications made up from services overseen by a third party can be delivered of the Cloud using PaaS. Offers elastic application scaling, enabling developers to create online services and applications with public, private and hybrid deployment options. PaaS provides pre-built tools, libraries and development environments.

e.g. AWS Lambda, Google App Engine, Google Cloud, IBM Cloud

SaaS is a cloud computing model that delivers software application over the internet.

e.g. Google workspace apps, Microsoft 365, Trello, Zoom, Slack, Adobe Creative Cloud

![](data:image/png;base64,)

### Single tenancy

Each customer has own dedicated instance of the software and supporting infrastructure.

### Multi-tenancy

Resources are shared amongst clients. e.g. might have own virtual machine but share the same physical machine. SaaS storage would be shared amongst clients, software would manage privacy and isolation.

[| Multi-Tenant vs Single-Tenant VMware Private Cloud | Liquid Web | Fellows , 2022 |](https://www.liquidweb.com/blog/multi-tenant-vs-single-tenant/#:~:text=What%20is%20a%20Single%2DTenant,hypervisor%20on%20a%20dedicated%20server)  

[| Private Cloud Computing | OTAVA | Owen, 2019 |](https://www.otava.com/reference/public-vs-private-cloud-computing/#:~:text=Private%20cloud%20hosting%20by%20definition,a%20single%20client%20or%20company)

[| Ring Central n.d. | Multi-Tenant Cloud |](https://www.ringcentral.co.uk/gb/en/blog/definitions/what-is-multi-tenant-cloud/)

[| Dingle Tenant vs Multi-Tenant Architecture | bmc | Raza, 2022 |](https://www.bmc.com/blogs/single-tenant-vs-multi-tenant/)

From <[https://app.qa.com/course/topic-1-cloud-computing-fundamentals-part-1-service-and-deployment-models-ai/cloud-service-models/?context_id=15065&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/course/topic-1-cloud-computing-fundamentals-part-1-service-and-deployment-models-ai/cloud-service-models/?context_id=15065&context_resource=lp)>

## Cloud design objectives

Main design objectives

1. Shifting computing from desktops to data centres
2. Service provisioning and cloud economics
3. Scalability in performance.
4. Data privacy protection
5. High quality Cloud services. Quality of service(QoS) must be standardised to make clouds interoperable
6. New standards and interfaces. Lock in should be avoided. Universally accepted api and access protocols are needed to provide high portability and flexibility.

## Cloud architecture

Physical servers linked as clusters - where virtual servers are created. A physical server can support many virtual machines. The virtual machines can be hired out as IaaS, or used in PaaS or SaaS.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Nodes in a virtual cluster can be virtual or physical.

VM on PM enables sharing of multiple functionalities

VMs are replicated across servers to enable parallelisation, fault tolerance and disaster recovery

VM clusters can be used as

- compute nodes
- Gateway nodes to provide access to the service
- Monitoring nodes to assess usage
- Scheduling nodes to assign jobs to VMs
- Nodes to spin up or wind down VMs as demand dictates

## Different types of cloud

1. Public
2. Private
3. Community
4. Hybrid

[7 Different Types of Cloud Computing Structures | UniPrint.net](https://uniprint.net/en/7-types-cloud-computing-structures/)

Public is built over the internet and can be accessed by anyone who pays. Most offer free layer for trial, e.g. Google App Engine, AWS, Azure, IB Smart Cloud, Salesforce Sales Cloud

Community clouds are collaborative infrastructure shared by multiple organisations, with some common or social business interest. Driven by need for shared data, shared services, shared industry regulations.

Private clouds are either on-premises or provided by a third party. On-prem is built within an organisation's own intranet. Could be cost effective or provide better functionality. Third party would be a single tenancy with a cloud provider. No hardware would be shared with other tenants.

Hybrid Private clouds that draw on computing capacity from external public clouds.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

## Mash up loud services

Can use multiple clouds

- Different models and types of clouds may be used for different functions
- Different clouds belonging to different parties required for a transaction
- Individual users and organisational user demand different services

![](data:image/png;base64,)

- Adewojo, A. A and Bass, J. M. (2018)"Evaluating the Effect of Multi-Tenancy Patterns in Containerized Cloud-Hosted Content Management System," 26th Euromicro International Conference on Parallel, Distributed and Network-based Processing (PDP), pp. 278-282, doi: 10.1109/PDP2018.2018.00047.
- Dey, H., Islam, R., and Arif, H. "An Integrated Model To Make Cloud Authentication And Multi-Tenancy More Secure," 2019 International Conference on Robotics,Electrical and Signal Processing Techniques (ICREST), 2019, pp. 502-506, doi: 10.1109/ICREST.2019.8644077.
- Slingerland, C. (2023) “Single-Tenant Vs, Multi-Tenant Cloud: When to use each”, Cloudzero, 22 September, Available at: [Single-Tenant Vs. Multi-Tenant Cloud: When To Use Each (](https://www.cloudzero.com/blog/single-tenant-vs-multi-tenant/#:~:text=Customization%20%E2%80%93%20More%20customization%20options%20are,from%20a%20single%2Dtenant%20architecture.)[cloudzero.com](https://cloudzero.com/)[)](https://www.cloudzero.com/blog/single-tenant-vs-multi-tenant/#:~:text=Customization%20%E2%80%93%20More%20customization%20options%20are,from%20a%20single%2Dtenant%20architecture.)  (Accessed 15-07-2023).
- Raza, M. (2022) “Single Tennant vs Multi-Tenant Architecture”, bmcblogs, 11 February, Available at: [Single Tenant vs Multi-Tenant Architecture – BMC Software | Blogs](https://www.bmc.com/blogs/single-tenant-vs-multi-tenant/) (Accessed:25 March 2022).
- Ring Central (n.d.) Multi-Tenant Cloud, Available at: [What is a Multi-Tenant Cloud? - Definition, Platforms & Security | RingCentral Blog UK](https://www.ringcentral.co.uk/gb/en/blog/definitions/what-is-multi-tenant-cloud/), (Accessed:25 March 2022).

From <[https://app.qa.com/course/topic-1-cloud-computing-fundamentals-part-1-service-and-deployment-models-ai/references-and-links/?context_id=15065&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/course/topic-1-cloud-computing-fundamentals-part-1-service-and-deployment-models-ai/references-and-links/?context_id=15065&context_resource=lp)>

## Scalability, Convergence and Applications

### Parallel, distributed and centralised computing

Parallel computing, processors are used in parallel. Memory might be shared(processors tightly coupled) or memory may be distributed(processors loosely coupled). Breaking down larger problems into smaller independent parts simultaneously executed by multiple processors communicating via shared memory.

Distributed computing. Multiple autonomous systems collaborate on a single problem or task - servers networked as a cluster. Computers can be close together and connected by a local network, or far apart and connected by a wide area network.

Centralised computing, resources are centralised on one physical system. Many data sentres and supercomputers are centralised systems but are used in parallel, distributed and cloud computing applications. They may also use parallelism and distribution within a centralised service.

Cloud computing  can be centralised or distributed depending on context of viewpoint. Sometimes called utility computing or service computing.

- Concurrent computing - parallel and distributed computing
- Ubiquitous computing - computing for pervasive devices wired or wireless
- Internet computing - any type of computing over the internet

There have been efforts to create computation grids or data grids, like P2P for science or file sharing. Mainly community oriented than commercial.

### High-throughput Computing(HTC) and High-performance Computing(HPC)

HTC focuses on throughput - number of tasks computed per unit time. Normally simple tasks.

HPC concentrates on speed and undertaking complex mathematical problems. Uses supercomputers and parallel processing techniques to solve complex computation problems that require significant amount of computation power.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Both HTC and HPC demand multi-core processors that can handle hundreds or thousands of computer threads. They both emphasise parallelism, distributed computing, and the requirement to satisfy the huge demand for compute power in terms of throughput, efficiency, scalability and reliability.

![This table shows the classification of types of distributed/parallel systems. Select the link that follows to download an accessible version.](data:image/png;base64,)

### Features and metrics for cloud

- Scalability
- Elasticity
- Availability, uptime, MTBF+MTTR [mean time between failure(MTBF), mean time to repair(MTTR)]
- Reliability, Probability of failure, MTBF/(MTBF+MTTR)
- Efficiency,
- Security

Metrics

- Availability, uptime, MTBF+MTTR [mean time between failure(MTBF), mean time to repair(MTTR)]
- Reliability, Probability of failure, MTBF/(MTBF+MTTR)
- Response time, time to respond to a request
- Security. Number of incidents or threats during a time period
- Throughput, number of transaction processed over a time period
- Capacity, size of workload compared to available infrastructure
- Scalability, Percentage of overall capacity that can be used without failure or degradation of service, number of clients that can be served without degradation, number of increase in requests that can be tolerated without degradation,
- Latency, time between submitting a packet and its arrival at destination
- Service and helpdesk, number of request per time period
- Cost per customer

Here are some articles which discuss metrics for cloud:

[| Metrics that matter in cloud application monitoring | TechTarget | Tozzi (2021) |](https://www.techtarget.com/searchcloudcomputing/feature/Metrics-that-matter-in-cloud-application-monitoring)

[| What is Cloud Performance and How to Implement it in Your Organization | NetApp (2021) |](https://cloud.netapp.com/blog/azure-anf-blg-what-is-cloud-performance)

[| The 25 Most Important Cloud Metrics For SaaS Companies To Monitor | Cloud Zero (2021) |](https://www.cloudzero.com/blog/cloud-metrics)

[| Cloud Monitoring 101: Metrics, KPIs, and more in 2021 | Data Floo | Bari (2021) |](https://datafloq.com/read/cloud-monitoring-101-metrics-kpis-2021/)

[Cloud Monitoring: Choosing the Right Metrics | bmc | Raza (2021) |](https://www.bmc.com/blogs/cloud-monitoring/)

From <[https://app.qa.com/course/topic-1-cloud-computing-fundamentals-part-2-scalability-convergence-and-applications-ai-1698/features-and-metrics-for-cloud/?context_id=15065&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/course/topic-1-cloud-computing-fundamentals-part-2-scalability-convergence-and-applications-ai-1698/features-and-metrics-for-cloud/?context_id=15065&context_resource=lp)>

### Scalability and elasticity

Scalability is the ability to meet increasing demand, new VMs(horizontal) or larger VMS(vertical)

HTC - more servers can cope with demand

HPC - higher number of processors are used in a complex calculation.

Elasticity the ability to shrink or grow dynamically. Only pay for what is needed.

### Amdahl's Law

P proportion of program that can be parallised

1-P part that cannot

N - number of processors

![SpeedUp (P,N) = 1 over (1–P) + P over N](data:image/png;base64,)

![A line graph illustrating Amdahl’s law as described in the text.](data:image/png;base64,)

### Gustafson's Law

S = 1-P serial part

The parallel part scales linearly with the amount of resources, but the serial part does not increase with respect to the size of the problem.

Scaled speedup = s + pN

 1=s+π= execution time on a parallel computer, with π = parallel part. —

Ss(n)=(s+ πn)/(s+ π)=n+(1-n)s

![A line graph illustrating Gustafson’s law as described in the text.](data:image/png;base64,)

[Scalability: strong and weak scaling – PDC Blog](https://www.kth.se/blogs/pdc/2018/11/scalability-strong-and-weak-scaling/)

### Availability and reliability

Availability is the percent of time the system is available

Reliability is the probability of failure

Availability metrics are MTTF and MTTR and Cluster availability = MTTF/(MTTF+MTTR)

Clusters should be designed without a single point of failure

PM can be replaced by a PM, but VM can more quickly replace a VM.

### Cloud service time

Cloud service time is determined by the task waiting time.

Such as

- Task response time
- Task waiting time
- Task execution time

SLA includes promised time to complete a service.

If a cloud service is at capacity, jobs may have to wait

M/M/c is a multi-server queuing model that describes a system where arrivals form a single que and are governed by a Poisson process(with c servers), and job service times are exponentially distributed.

![P(k events in interval) = e minus lambda lambda K over K!](data:image/png;base64,)

[Poisson Distributions | Definition, Formula & Examples](https://www.scribbr.co.uk/stats/poisson-distribution-meaning/)

Lambda is the mean number of events.

K is the number of times the event occurs

### Efficiency

Cost - cost per hour

Time - how long it takes to complete a task

Energy usage - kWh

Also cost benefit approach

## Convergence and new applications

SMACT

Social

Mobile

Analytics

Cloud

IoT

![Convergence of Big Data Analytics as described in the text.](data:image/png;base64,)

Big data - vast volumes of structured and unstructured data

Volume

Velocity

Variety

Need to use Hadoop and Spark to deal with large data.

### Cognitive and neuromorphic computing

Cognitive science includes psychology, AI, neuroscience, and linguistics. From low level machine learning to high level neuromorphic circuitry to brain-like computers.

Neuroinformatics combines informatics research and brain modelling.

Neuromorphic computing started in 1980s, the idea was to create very large scale integration(VLSI) to create circuits which mimic neurobiology architectures.

e.g. modelling connections as neurons in ML.

![Modelling connections as neurons as described in the text.](data:image/png;base64,)

Spiking neural networks(SNN) are NN that more closely mimic natural neural networks. Transmit only when a threshold is reached.

Can transfer learning from NN to SNN.

Real world uses

- Speech understanding
- Sentiment analysis
- Face recognition
- Election insights
- Autonomous driving
- Deep learning applications

There is a drive to design new chips for neuromorphic computing and cognitive computing. Such as Tensor Processing Units(TPU) and Neural Processing Units(NPU)

- IBM TrueNorth Chip
- IBM Neurocomputer
- Google TPU in TensorFlow

- Baker, K. and Ritts, M. (2018) “Smart Earth: a meta-review and implications for environmental governance”, Global Environmental Change, 52, September, pp201-211, Doi:  10.1016/j.gloenvcha.2018.07.011, Available at: [Smart Earth: A meta-review and implications for environmental governance – ScienceDirect](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0959378017313730) (Accessed: 29-03-2022).

### Reflect

Understanding metrics better can be useful when going for cloud devices.

If you haven’t already, read the chapter/section below from the module reading list to learn about this topic:

     Cognitive Applications, MIT Press.
